cases.title: Scenario matrix row for session close blocking self check-in
preconditions: A class session exists and is transitioned from Scheduled to Open by assigned Lecturer.
Student is actively enrolled in the session class section.
steps: Transition the same session to Closed via session lifecycle service path.
Submit a student check-in command referencing a valid token previously issued for that session.
Query attendance records for the student and session.
cases.expected: The check-in result is rejected with SessionClosed and no new successful attendance record is created for that student-session pair.

cases.title: Close session transaction persists state and blocks subsequent success writes
preconditions: Real database transaction is enabled for session and attendance modules.
Session is Open with at least one enrolled student without a success check-in yet.
steps: Execute POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close through the application service boundary.
Immediately execute POST /v1/check-ins for the enrolled student against the same session token context.
Read persisted Session, CheckInAttempt, and AttendanceRecord rows for this student and session.
cases.expected: Session state is persisted as Closed, failed attempt is recorded with SessionClosed, and no new Present/Late success row is inserted after closure.

cases.title: Illegal check-in after Closed state transition
preconditions: Session currently in Open state and then moved to Closed.
Student actor and enrollment are valid for the class section.
steps: Capture beforeState as Closed after invoking close transition.
Invoke check-in command for the closed session.
Capture afterState and attendance mutation outcome.
cases.expected: beforeState remains Closed and afterState remains Closed with no attendance success mutation; the check-in path returns SessionClosed.

cases.title: HTTP contract for close then student check-in denial
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer and enrolled Student accounts are authenticated.
A Scheduled class session exists for lecturer scope.
steps: Lecturer calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open and receives 200 with data.state Open.
Lecturer calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close and receives 200 with data.state Closed.
Student calls POST /v1/check-ins with qrToken bound to the same session.
Inspect HTTP status and response envelope fields data/meta/error.
cases.expected: Student request is rejected as a rule failure with error.code SessionClosed in the standard envelope and no successful attendance outcome is returned.

cases.title: RBAC denial when non-lecturer roles attempt session close
preconditions: One Open session exists in lecturer-owned section.
Student and ITAdmin users are authenticated.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
ITAdmin calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Lecturer in assigned scope calls the same endpoint as control.
cases.expected: Student and ITAdmin receive authorization denial (403 Forbidden or OutOfScope) while assigned Lecturer succeeds; denied calls do not alter session state.

cases.title: Boundary ordering: duplicate check-in after closure still resolves to SessionClosed
preconditions: Student already has one successful check-in in an Open session.
Session is then closed by assigned Lecturer.
steps: Student re-submits POST /v1/check-ins after session is Closed.
Capture returned error.code and status.
Verify successful attendance row count for student-session remains one.
cases.expected: API returns SessionClosed for post-close attempts and no additional successful attendance record is created.

cases.title: UI journey shows closed-state gating for student check-in
preconditions: Lecturer UI can open and close a session for an assigned section.
Student opens check-in page/session URL in supported mobile browser profile.
steps: Lecturer opens session from session list and confirms Open badge/indicator is visible.
Lecturer closes the same session and verifies session status badge changes to Closed.
Student attempts to submit check-in from check-in form after closure.
Observe UI error message, disabled or blocked submission path, and absence of success confirmation.
cases.expected: Browser flow reflects Closed state and prevents successful student check-in, surfacing SessionClosed-equivalent feedback without showing a success state.
